claim: Focus on four core components to build agents: large language model, system prompt (agent instructions), tools, and memory—everything else can wait
actionable: Start building agents by picking an LLM, writing a basic system prompt, adding one tool, and setting up conversation memory before considering observability or security.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Use Claude Haiku 4.5 for prototyping agents cheaply and Claude Sonnet 4.5 as the all-around best model, but don't worry about perfect model selection at the start
actionable: Start with Claude Haiku 4.5 when prototyping to save tokens, upgrade to Sonnet 4.5 when you have a working agent and need better performance.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Keep your agent tools to under 10 with distinct purposes—overlapping tools confuse the model and cause it to forget to use tools or use wrong ones
actionable: Audit your agent tools regularly and remove or consolidate any with overlapping functionality, keeping the total below 10 tools.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: System prompts for agents should include persona, goals, tool instructions with examples, output format, and miscellaneous instructions—keep it under a few hundred lines
actionable: Use a system prompt template with these sections: persona (couple sentences), goals (couple sentences), tool instructions, output format, and catch-all miscellaneous instructions.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is essential for 80% of agents in production—enable agents to search documents and ground responses in real data
actionable: Add RAG capability to your agents early by giving them tools to search a knowledge base or document store to ground their responses in real information.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Limit short-term memory to the last 10-20 messages for longer conversations to manage context and reduce token usage
actionable: Implement a sliding window that only includes the most recent 10-20 conversation messages in your context to avoid token bloat.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Use guardrails to limit what information goes into prompts (block PII, API keys) and validate outputs to prevent harmful responses
actionable: Implement guardrails using tools like Guardrails AI to filter PII from inputs and validate model outputs before users see them.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Never hardcode API keys—always use environment variables to store sensitive credentials securely
actionable: Move all API keys, credentials, and secrets from your code to environment variables before deploying any agent to production.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Use MCP servers to quickly add prepackaged tool sets to your agent without building them from scratch
actionable: Search for MCP servers that match your agent's domain and integrate them early to avoid reinventing tool functionality.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Set up observability from day one using tools like Langfuse to track token usage, latency, and tool calls for debugging and monitoring
actionable: Initialize Langfuse or similar observability tool in your agent code early so you can track performance metrics and troubleshoot issues.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes

claim: Package agents in Docker containers from the start to prepare them for cloud deployment and serverless environments
actionable: Build your agent to run in a Docker container from the beginning so deployment to cloud platforms is straightforward.
source_title: Learn 90% of Building AI Agents in 30 Minutes
